column1: # SlopDrive-32 - OTA partition table (16MB Arduino Nano ESP32 / NORA-W106)
# BYTE-IDENTICAL to the Arduino-bundled default_16MB.csv the device already
# runs. Because every offset and size is unchanged
# that installs the first OTA-capable firmware does NOT relocate NVS or the
# LittleFS (spiffs) region - saved config AND the web-UI image both survive the
# migration. We vendor it into the repo (instead of relying on the framework's
# built-in) so the layout is version-controlled and cannot silently change when
# the arduino-esp32 package updates.
# Layout math (16MB = 0x1000000):
#   nvs      0x5000   (20 KB)   - persisted config (RangeMapper
#   otadata  0x2000   (8 KB)    - active-slot selector for the bootloader
#   ota_0    0x640000 (6.5 MB)  - app slot A; current app ~1.54MB => 4.2x headroom
#   ota_1    0x640000 (6.5 MB)  - app slot B; A/B ping-pong OTA target
#   spiffs   0x360000 (3.375MB) - LittleFS web bundle (current image ~3.37MB)
#   coredump 0x10000  (64 KB)   - crash coredump region (preserved from current table)
# Name
nvs
otadata
app0
app1
spiffs
coredump
